svg.text: The background is the model's forecast, each dot is a run's actual
move: where the colors agree, the model is right
one dot per run · 67 modelable runs from experiments/spread_util_unified.json (round-1 record per run)
Background = the model's ONE-round forecast move ρ·σ on the dots' color scale; dots are the
WHOLE-trajectory move — compare sign and relative magnitude, not absolute darkness.
round-1 agreement ρ = correlation of judge scores with candidate value scores (−1 disagree → +1 lockstep)
round-1 spread σ = within-prompt SD of value scores
Dot fill = the run's OBSERVED endpoint
one-round move = agreement ρ × spread σ
Does dot color match the background?
39 of the 50 runs that moved ≥ 0.15 sit on a background of
the matching color — i.e. the sign of the observed move
equals the sign of the forecast ρ·σ.
